response: In C# it is possible to measure how long it takes to send a UDP packet to a server but not the full round trip like a ping. You can record the time before sending the packet and then calculate the time difference once the packet is sent. This gives you the one way transmission time. To do this you can use the System.Diagnostics.Stopwatch class. This method does not account for any delays on the server side. It only measures the time from when the packet is sent until it is transmitted. This approach can be useful for certain network diagnostics. The Stopwatch provides high resolution timing which is suitable for this purpose. You can start the stopwatch before sending the packet and stop it immediately after. This will give you the duration it took to send the packet. Keep in mind that UDP is connectionless and does not guarantee delivery. So this method only gives you the time taken to send the packet not whether it was received.
